agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement the Order Fulfillment state management feature. Bird has defined the domain invariants governing order state transitions and refund eligibility. MJ has designed the architecture: a PATCH endpoint for state changes, an OrderStateMachine for transition validation, and a separate RefundService that verifies order state before issuing refunds. Your job is to implement all three components with the domain rules baked in as hard constraints.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"An order cannot transition from SHIPPED to PENDING","testable_assertion":"Calling transitionState(SHIPPED, PENDING) on OrderStateMachine throws/returns ORDER_STATE_INVALID"},{"rule":"A refund can only be issued on a DELIVERED order","testable_assertion":"RefundService.issueRefund(orderId) rejects with an error when the order state is anything other than DELIVERED"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Expose a PATCH /orders/{id}/state endpoint for state transitions","implementation_note":"The endpoint receives the target state in the request body. Delegate all transition validation to OrderStateMachine before persisting. Return 409 Conflict with error code ORDER_STATE_INVALID when a transition is rejected."},{"decision":"State transitions validated in an OrderStateMachine class","implementation_note":"OrderStateMachine owns the full map of legal transitions. It is a pure domain component with no I/O — it takes (currentState, targetState) and returns valid/invalid. This keeps the transition logic testable in isolation."},{"decision":"Refund logic lives in a separate RefundService","implementation_note":"RefundService calls the order service (or repository) to fetch current order state and asserts state == DELIVERED before proceeding. This enforces the domain invariant at the service boundary, not just the database level."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Reject invalid state transition from SHIPPED to PENDING","given":"An order in SHIPPED state","when":"A PATCH /orders/{id}/state request attempts to set state to PENDING","then":"The request is rejected with HTTP 409 and error code ORDER_STATE_INVALID"},{"criterion":"Refund only allowed on DELIVERED orders","given":"An order in any state other than DELIVERED","when":"RefundService.issueRefund is called for that order","then":"The refund is rejected with an error indicating the order must be in DELIVERED state"},{"criterion":"Refund succeeds on DELIVERED order","given":"An order in DELIVERED state","when":"RefundService.issueRefund is called for that order","then":"The refund is issued successfully"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Order state invariant","technical_term":"State transition guard / validation rule in OrderStateMachine","definition":"A business rule that prohibits certain state transitions. Enforced in code as a guard check before any state mutation is persisted."},{"domain_term":"ORDER_STATE_INVALID","technical_term":"Error code returned in API response body (HTTP 409)","definition":"The error code surfaced when a caller attempts a state transition that violates a domain invariant."}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are explicit and unambiguous","Architecture components map cleanly to domain invariants","Acceptance criteria directly testable"],"low_confidence_areas":["Full set of valid state transitions not specified — only the SHIPPED->PENDING prohibition is explicit. Shaq may need to ask Bird for the complete transition map if implementing a whitelist approach."],"assumptions":["OrderStateMachine uses an allowlist or denylist of transitions — Bird's rules imply at minimum a denylist but a full allowlist would be safer","HTTP 409 Conflict is the appropriate status code for rejected state transitions (not specified by either agent, inferred from REST conventions)"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement the Order Fulfillment state management feature. Bird has defined the domain invariants governing order state transitions and refund eligibility. MJ has designed the architecture: a PATCH endpoint for state changes, an OrderStateMachine for transition validation, and a separate RefundService that verifies order state before issuing refunds. Your job is to implement all three components with the domain rules baked in as hard constraint

content.thinking:

No contradictions or missing outputs — both inputs are consistent and complementary.

Classification: `none`
